title: Unit 3 Hyper-Exhaustive Masterclass: Being & Having (Είμαι & Έχω)

script.text: Welcome... to the Hyper-Exhaustive Grammar Masterclass for Unit 3: Being and Having. To build real sentences in Greek... you need verbs. And if you only ever learn two verbs... they should be 'EE-me' for 'To Be'... and 'É-kho' for 'To Have'. They are the keys to a thousand conversations. Athina... where do we start with these building blocks?
Γεια σας! Before we meet the verbs... we must meet the actors. The Personal Pronouns. This is how we say 'I'... 'You'... 'He'... and so on. Let's learn them clearly.
Read the singular ones first... the actors on stage by themselves.
Εγώ (e-ghó) for I. Εσύ (e-sí) for you. Αυτός, Αυτή, Αυτό (af-tós, af-tí, af-tó) for He... She... and It.

I noticed that 'af-tós' and his friends share the same endings as the nouns we learned in Unit 2... 'os', 'ee', 'o'. It’s all very consistent. Now... what about the groups? The 'We'... the 'You all'... and 'They'?

Listen to the rhyme: Εμείς (e-mís) for we. Εσείς (e-sís) for you. And for they... we have Αυτοί, Αυτές, Αυτά (af-tí, af-tés, af-tá). If 'e-mís' and 'e-sís' sound confusing... just remember they rhyming perfectly.
Now... this next point is one of the biggest 'Masterclass' tips. The 'Pro-Drop' rule. In English... we HAVE to say 'I' am a student. But in Greek? Athina?

In Greek... we drop the pronoun 90% of the time! You don't need 'e-ghó' because the ending of the verb already tells everyone it is YOU. If you say 'e-ghó eée-me'... you are being very emphatic... like saying... 'I specifically am the one'. Normally... just say 'eée-me e-thó'... 'I am here'.

Drop the pronouns to sound like a native. Got it. Now... let's conjugate our two champions. First: 'EE-me'... to be. This is used for identity... location... and states of being. Athina... take it away.
Είμαι (eée-me)... Είσαι (eée-se)... Είναι (eée-ne). Είμαστε (eée-mas-te)... Είστε (eée-ste)... Είναι (eée-ne).
Wait... 'eée-ne' was both 'he is' AND 'they are'? That's a 'Caution' in our lesson.
Yes! 'Είναι' handles both. You will always know which one it is because of the context... and because of the nouns that follow it. Don't worry... it becomes natural very fast.
And now... 'É-kho'... to have. Possession... age... family. This one is very regular but has a 'street' secret.
Έχω... έχεις... έχει. Έχουμε... έχετε... έχουν. But in the street... we often add an extra 'e' sound to 'é-khoun'. It becomes... 'é-khou-ne'. Both are correct... but 'é-khou-ne' is more relaxed.
Try to use 'é-khou-ne' when you're talking with friends. Now... what about the 'Respect' system? In English, 'You' is always 'You'. But in Greek... if I'm talking to my boss... or a stranger?

Never use 'e-sí'! Use the plural form 'e-sís' and the plural verb 'ée-ste' or 'é-khe-te'. Even if you are only talking to ONE person. It’s like 'Usted' in Spanish or 'Vous' in French. It shows respect and distance.
And finally... asking a question. How do we turn 'You have the book' into 'Do you have the book?'
No helper words! No 'Do' or 'Does'. Just raise your voice at the end. 'É-kheis to vi-vlee-o'... becomes... 'É-kheis to vi-vlee-o;'. And remember... write it with a semicolon!

You have successfully completed the exhaustive Unit 3 Masterclass. You now have the verbs to say who you are... what you have... and how to treat a stranger with respect. Review the everyday objects and places vocabulary to test your 'É-kho' skills. Excellent work... we’ll see you in Unit 4: The Engine Room of Greek Verbs.
Συγχαρητήρια! Τα λέμε στο επόμενο μάθημα.
